userStories.title: Analyze current campaign performance and identify failure points
userStories.description: Deep dive into wave_1_no_website_jan15 campaign (98 sent, 0 responses) to understand why response rate is 0%. Identify messaging, timing, targeting, or compliance issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Read campaign_analytics.json and outreach_history.json
Analyze SMS message templates used
Check delivery status and timing
Identify potential issues (message quality, timing, targeting, TCPA compliance)
Create analysis report in ralph/campaign_analysis.md
userStories.notes: Foundation for optimization - must understand why 0% response rate

userStories.title: Create pain-point-specific message templates
userStories.description: Create targeted templates for top 3 pain points: no_online_transactions (160 leads), few_reviews (104 leads), no_website (40 leads). Each template should be compelling and specific to the pain point.
acceptanceCriteria: Create template for no_online_transactions pain point
Create template for few_reviews pain point
Create template for no_website pain point
Each template under 160 chars (SMS limit)
Include personalization ({business_name}, {city})
Include clear CTA
TCPA compliant (business number, opt-out, sender ID)
Templates saved to templates/sms/ directory
userStories.notes: Targeted messaging should improve response rates

userStories.title: Implement A/B testing framework for message templates
userStories.description: Add ability to test multiple message variants against each other to find winning templates
acceptanceCriteria: Create a_b_test.py module in src/
Support splitting leads into control/variant groups
Track response rate per variant
Calculate statistical significance (85% confidence threshold)
Generate A/B test report showing winner
Minimum 50 contacts per variant before declaring winner

userStories.title: Add optimal send time scheduling based on business hours
userStories.description: Instead of sending all at once, schedule messages during business hours (Tue-Thu, 10 AM - 2 PM) when decision-makers are most likely to respond
acceptanceCriteria: Update outreach_scheduler.py with time-slot logic
Default schedule: Tue-Thu, 10 AM - 2 PM local time
Spread sends across multiple days (not all at once)
Avoid Mon morning, Fri afternoon, weekends
Store scheduled_send_time in outreach queue
Process queue respects scheduled times
userStories.notes: Timing can dramatically impact response rates

userStories.title: Implement automatic follow-up sequence for non-responders
userStories.description: After 3-5 days, send follow-up message to non-responders using different angle (social proof instead of pain point)
acceptanceCriteria: Update follow_up_sequences.json structure to support auto-enrollment
Auto-enroll leads in follow-up after initial message
Wait 3-5 days before follow-up
Use different message angle (social proof: 'Just helped 3 Naples gyms...')
Stop follow-up if lead responds or opts out
Max 2 follow-ups total (initial + 2 follow-ups = 3 touches)
Track which touch number got the response
userStories.notes: CHECKPOINT: Review follow-up logic before enabling automation. Risk of appearing spammy if done wrong.

userStories.title: Add response categorization and auto-routing to ClickUp
userStories.description: When leads respond, categorize their response (hot/warm/cold/question) and auto-create ClickUp tasks for hot leads
acceptanceCriteria: Add response categorization logic in sms_outreach.py
Categories: hot_lead, warm_lead, question, not_interested, wrong_number
Hot lead keywords: 'yes', 'interested', 'tell me more', 'call me'
Auto-create ClickUp task for hot_lead and question categories
Update campaign_analytics.py to track by response category
Don't create ClickUp tasks for not_interested or wrong_number

userStories.title: Create campaign performance dashboard script
userStories.description: Generate visual report showing campaign metrics: response rate by template, by pain point, by send time, conversion funnel
acceptanceCriteria: Add dashboard.py to src/
Show response rate by template (table)
Show response rate by pain point (table)
Show response rate by day of week and hour (heatmap data)
Show conversion funnel (contacted → responded → qualified → meeting → closed)
Identify top-performing template and recommend scaling it
Export to dashboard.txt in output/

metadata.objective: Optimize cold outreach from 0% to 3-5% response rate through targeted messaging, timing, and follow-up automation
